telltalePatterns: Syllable-timed rhythm with lack of vowel reduction

prosody.fluencyNotes: Speech is fluent but lacks native-like connected speech (linking, elision). Words are often pronounced distinctly, leading to a staccato quality.

strengths: Good command of grammar and vocabulary
Clear articulation of sibilant sounds /s/ and /z/ (in their native context)

recommendedFocus.reason: This is a high-frequency error that can cause misunderstanding between common words (e.g., 'west' vs 'vest').
Substituting these with /s/ and /z/ is a primary marker of the accent and affects many high-frequency words (e.g., 'the', 'think', 'with').

recommendedFocus.area: English stress-timed rhythm and vowel reduction
recommendedFocus.reason: Adopting a more stress-timed pattern and using weak forms/schwa in unstressed syllables will dramatically improve naturalness and flow.

result.transcript: Yesterday, the weather was really beautiful, so I decided to go for a walk through the park near my house. The trees were green and the birds were singing everywhere. I think spring is my favorite time of the year. I walked along the river and watched some children playing with a big red ball. They were laughing and having so much fun together. One little girl threw the ball very high and it landed right in the water. Her father had to reach in and pull it out. After that, I stopped at a small village shop to buy some bread and a bottle of juice.
